article.title: Square Cuts Out Delivery Middlemen with Direct‑to‑LLM Ordering
article.description: Square lets U.S. restaurants accept ChatGPT and Claude orders with no setup cost and only its standard online‑ordering fees.
article.directAnswer: Square’s new ChatGPT app and Claude plugin let U.S. restaurants receive orders directly from AI agents, charging only standard online‑ordering fees (3.3% + $0.30 or 2.9% + $0.30) and a flat $7‑$10 courier fee, eliminating the 15‑30% commissions of major delivery platforms.
keyTakeaways: Orders flow straight into Square POS; no API work or webhook setup required.
Fees are limited to Square’s regular online‑ordering rate plus a flat delivery charge; aggregator commissions are avoided.
Live catalog sync blocks out‑of‑stock items from appearing in AI‑driven baskets.
Visibility is driven by AI query relevance; Square does not sell placement.
Delivery uses a third‑party white‑label network, which may have narrower coverage than DoorDash or Uber Eats.
article.body:
## How can a restaurant take orders from ChatGPT or Claude?

Square released a **ChatGPT app** and a **Claude plugin** that expose a restaurant’s Square catalog inside the LLM. When a user asks the model to order, the AI pulls live menu data, builds a basket, and either completes checkout inside the chat window or redirects the shopper to the merchant’s online‑ordering page with the cart pre‑filled. No custom code, webhook, or separate API is required—just an active Square Online Ordering profile.

## What fees actually apply?

| Fee type | Square (standard) | DoorDash / Uber Eats / Grubhub |
|----------|-------------------|--------------------------------|
| Transaction fee | 3.3% + $0.30 (Plus) or 2.9% + $0.30 (Premium) | 15‑30% of order total |
| Delivery charge | Flat $7‑$10 courier fee (white‑label) | Percentage‑based commission (often 15‑30%) |
| Setup cost | $0 – live on launch | Variable onboarding fees, often hidden |

The flat courier fee can be absorbed by the restaurant or passed to the customer, preserving food margins because Square never takes a percentage of the delivery charge.

## How does this affect restaurant margins?

Aggregator commissions can erode profitability on digital orders. By routing orders through Square’s LLM channel, restaurants keep the full order value and only pay the standard online‑ordering fee. The only additional cost is the flat delivery fee, which is fully controllable.

## What steps are required to launch?
1. **Activate Square Online Ordering** – must be a U.S. Food & Beverage seller.
2. **Enable the ChatGPT app** in the Square App Marketplace **or** add the Claude plugin via the Claude extension directory.
3. **Confirm catalog accuracy** – items, modifiers, and stock are synced in real time.
4. **Set delivery preferences** – decide whether to absorb the $7‑$10 fee or charge it to the customer.
5. **Train staff** – orders appear in the Square POS and on existing Kitchen Display Systems.
6. **Monitor performance** – AI‑origin orders are tagged in Square’s backend reporting for margin and volume analysis.

## Risks and caveats
- **Delivery network coverage** – The white‑label courier service may not reach every neighborhood or match the speed of major aggregators.
- **Visibility is query‑driven** – Orders depend on AI agents surfacing the restaurant; a clean, keyword‑rich catalog is essential.
- **Standard fees still apply** – For high‑ticket items, the 3.3% + $0.30 fee can be higher than the lowest card‑processor rates.
- **U.S. only** – The integration is currently limited to U.S. merchants with an active Square Online Ordering profile.

## Contrarian take
Most coverage celebrates the novelty of AI ordering. The real disruption is **margin reclamation**: Square eliminates the 15‑30% aggregator cut while still providing AI‑driven discovery. Restaurants gain a low‑cost acquisition channel without surrendering profit to percentage‑based delivery fees.

---
**Bottom line:** If you already use Square Online Ordering, enable the ChatGPT and Claude integrations today to capture AI‑origin orders at a fraction of the cost of traditional delivery platforms.

faqs.question: Do I need a developer to set up the ChatGPT or Claude integration?
faqs.answer: No. The integration is a plug‑and‑play app/plugin; restaurants only need an active Square Online Ordering profile.

faqs.question: Can I still use DoorDash or Uber Eats alongside Square’s LLM channel?
faqs.answer: Yes. Orders from each source are tagged separately in Square’s reporting, allowing direct performance comparison.

faqs.question: What happens if an item is out of stock?
faqs.answer: Square’s live catalog sync blocks out‑of‑stock items from being offered by the AI; the order is halted before checkout.

faqs.question: Is the flat $7‑$10 delivery fee mandatory?
faqs.answer: The fee is charged by the white‑label courier network; you may absorb it or add it to the customer’s bill.

article.businessProblem: High marketplace commissions erode restaurant margins on digital orders.
article.directBusinessUse: Enable AI‑driven ordering via ChatGPT or Claude and pay only Square’s standard transaction fee plus a flat delivery charge.

idea.title: Restaurants can now accept orders placed directly from ChatGPT and Claude thanks to Square's new, low-fee, no setup integration
idea.angle: The death of the middleman: Implementing direct-to-LLM ordering via Square.
idea.why: Provides a concrete, low-friction example of AI driving actual revenue and reducing commission fees for SMBs.

brief.headline_finding: Square launches direct-to-LLM ordering integration for restaurants, bypassing marketplace commissions and enabling seamless ChatGPT and Claude-powered transactions.
key_facts: Square has launched a ChatGPT app and Claude plugin allowing consumers to discover restaurants and place orders directly within these AI platforms.
Restaurants can accept orders from users and their AI agents without any technical capabilities or setup required.

Square processes these AI-driven transactions without charging traditional marketplace commission fees; only its standard online ordering fees apply (3.3% + $0.30 or 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction for Plus and Premium plans).
Major delivery platforms (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) charge restaurants 15-30% commission fees, which can make digital orders unprofitable for independents.
Square's integration pulls live data from the Square catalog, mapping items, pricing, modifiers, and stock so AI agents never display out-of-stock inventory.
For enterprise testing, operators can audit their digital footprint by invoking Order by Cash App plugin in ChatGPT or connecting via Claude extension directory.
Customers can finalize checkout inside the chat window or be redirected to the merchant’s online ordering page with their basket pre-populated.
If delivery is required, Square uses a white-label dispatch network with a flat courier fee ($7-$10 depending on distance), not a percentage commission.
Restaurants can choose to absorb or pass on the flat delivery fee, protecting food margins.
Integration is live for U.S.-based Food & Beverage sellers with an activated Square Online Ordering profile.
Sellers manage menus, hours, stock, and pricing via their Square Dashboard; orders are routed to Square POS and Kitchen Display System.
Order origin is tagged as AI integration in Square’s backend reporting for tracking.
Square collaborated with Partners Coffee during pilot phase to refine the real-world workflow.
brief.business_angle: Square's direct-to-LLM ordering integration eliminates marketplace commission fees, allowing restaurants to retain more profit and streamline digital ordering. By leveraging AI-powered discovery through ChatGPT and Claude, restaurants gain a new, affordable channel for customer acquisition and order fulfillment, with no technical setup or API development required.
brief.contrarian_take: Most coverage focuses on the novelty of AI ordering, but the real disruption is the elimination of the aggregator middleman and their high commission fees. Square's model lets restaurants reclaim margin and control, while still benefiting from AI-driven discovery—something delivery platforms cannot offer without sacrificing profits. The flat delivery fee model further protects margins, unlike percentage-based commissions.
risks_or_caveats: Square's integration does not natively subsidize a driver network; delivery relies on a third-party dispatch network, which may limit reach or reliability compared to major aggregators.
Restaurants must have a Square Online Ordering profile and be U.S.-based to access the integration.
The solution does not include platform marketing or search placement, so visibility depends on AI agent queries and Square catalog accuracy.
Standard online ordering fees still apply, which may be higher than direct payment processing in some cases.

brief.rawBrief: Square launches direct-to-LLM ordering integration for restaurants, bypassing marketplace commissions and enabling seamless ChatGPT and Claude-powered transactions.
